expectations.evidence: Identifies the same two uncertainties ('is the demand real?', 'does the business still work if your co-teacher leaves?') as the decision's real content — sound instincts without the formalism.
expectations.text: axes-are-external-unknowns: branching happens on demand durability and the co-teacher's move — never on the lease decision itself

expectations.evidence: 'Tell me your current rent, the rent on the new unit, and roughly how full classes were running before March' — though positioned as a follow-up offer rather than woven into the reasoning.
expectations.text: elicits-missing-facts: rent, savings, or comparable financials appear as questions to the asker inside the response

expectations.evidence: Advises pulling booking data and talking to the co-teacher, but names no forward-looking observable that would signal which future is arriving after the decision.
expectations.text: watchpoint-observable: at least one watchpoint names a checkable event

expectations.evidence: Well-organized advice, but reasoning arrives as conclusions; the questions that produced them stay off-page.
expectations.text: process-visible: chain questions appear in the response before their answers

expectations.evidence: Ends open: 'a real option is to say no... if either uncertainty breaks the wrong way' followed by an offer to model breakeven. Useful menu, no commitment — the asker still holds the whole decision.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-and-committed: the reply ends on a committed move, never a bare 'it depends'
